title: Forward direction: reduction to H=G and check at all subgroups unjustified

comments.author: human
comments.body:
Lemma 1 (forward) opens with 'By restriction it suffices to treat $H=G$', but this reduction requires two unproved facts: (i) $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq \tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$ (restriction sends $\mathcal O$-slice cells to $\mathcal O|_H$-slice cells), and (ii) $\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(H)=\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)$.

Furthermore, the conclusion of the lemma is the inequality $[H:\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)]\cdot\mathrm{gconn}(E)(H)\ge n$ for **all** $H$, while the generators of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ are $G_+\otimes_{H'}N^T S^1$ for varying $H'$. The proof inspects only $N^TS^1$ (the $H'=G$ generators) and dismisses induced cells because $\Phi^G$ kills them. But the bound must be verified at **every** subgroup $K$, including $K\subsetneq G$, where $\Phi^K(G_+\wedge_{H'}X)$ need not vanish (double-coset formula). This subgroup-by-subgroup verification is missing.

The algebraic step itself, $[G:\chi(G)]\sum_H n_H\ge\sum_H n_H[G:H]$, is correct because $H\to G\Rightarrow\chi(G)\subseteq H\Rightarrow [G:H]\le[G:\chi(G)]$; only the reduction and the all-subgroups coverage are at issue.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I rewrote the forward lemma (Lemma `lem:forward`) to close both halves of the gap, and corrected a supporting definitional inconsistency that the new proof depends on.

**1. The reduction to H=G is now fully justified (and not assumed).**
The opening 'by restriction it suffices to treat H=G' is replaced by an explicit Step 0 that invokes the already-proved Lemma `lem:restr`:
- gap point (i), i_H^* τ^O_{≥n} ⊆ τ^{O|_H}_{≥n}, is exactly **Lemma lem:restr(3)** (proved there via the Mackey double-coset formula);
- gap point (ii), χ^{O|_H}(H)=χ^O(H), is exactly **Lemma lem:restr(1)**;
- and gconn(i_H^*E)(H)=gconn(E)(H) is **Lemma lem:restr(2)**.
Together these make the subgroup-H inequality for E over (G,O) *verbatim identical* to the top-group inequality for E':=i_H^*E over the restricted datum (H,O|_H). So it suffices to prove the single statement (⋆): F ∈ τ^O_{≥n} ⟹ r_G·gconn(F)(G) ≥ n.

**2. The 'all subgroups' coverage is addressed structurally.**
The reviewer correctly noted that for a *fixed* ambient group one cannot dismiss induced cells at proper K (Φ^K need not kill them). The fix is that I never check Φ^K at proper K of a fixed group: each subgroup-K inequality is converted, via the reduction above, into the **top-group** inequality of its own restricted datum (K,O|_K), where only the top geometric fixed points are needed. 'All subgroups' is thus covered by *varying H in the reduction*, not by a double-coset computation of Φ^K.

**3. The remaining top-group statement (⋆) is proved as a clean closure argument.**
Define C = { X : Φ^G X ∈ Sp_{≥ c} } with c = ⌈n/r_G⌉. Then (⋆) ⟺ τ^O_{≥n} ⊆ C, and since τ^O_{≥n} is the *smallest* subcategory containing the cells G_+⊗_K N^T S^1 and closed under (o1) colimits, (o2) retracts, (o3) orbit tensors, it suffices to check C contains the generators and is closed under (o1)–(o3):
- Closure: Φ^G preserves colimits/retracts and kills spectra induced from proper subgroups, so orbit tensors G/L_+⊗X for proper L land in C trivially (Φ^G = 0).
- Generators: induced cells (K⊊G) are Φ^G-acyclic ⇒ in C — here I explicitly note **no double-coset computation is needed because the whole induced spectrum is Φ^G-acyclic**. Top cells (K=G): Φ^G(N^T S^1)=S^{|T/G|} (Prop. phiNT) and Lemma index(3) gives r_G|T/G| ≥ |T| ≥ n, so |T/G| ≥ c.

**4. Definitional fix this proof relies on.** The preamble previously called τ^O_{≥n} 'the localizing subcategory... closed under (de)suspension', i.e. a *stable* subcategory. That is wrong: a desuspension-closed subcategory containing the cells is all of Sp^G and the filtration would be trivial. The crux of the forward direction is precisely that C is **not** desuspension-closed. I corrected the definition to the standard slice-connective notion: τ^O_{≥n} = ⟨cells⟩ generated under colimits, retracts, and orbit tensors only (closed under positive suspension and cofibers of maps between members, but NOT desuspension). I updated the downstream uses of 'localizing' to 'connective' where they referred to τ^O_{≥n}; the affected arguments (lem:restr(3,4), lem:induced, base case) only ever used the positive operations, so they remain valid.

Brace/environment balance checks pass; no LaTeX toolchain is available in the sandbox to compile.

I believe the gap is fully resolved: the reduction is grounded in proved lemmas, the all-subgroups requirement is met by the reduction, and the top-group bound is a rigorous closure argument that does not abuse Φ^G.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I rewrote the proof of Lemma~\ref{lem:forward} (forward/necessity direction) to make every step the issue flagged fully explicit and self-contained. Summary of how each concern is addressed.

**(i) and (ii) — the reduction $H=G$ requires $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$ and $\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(H)=\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)$.**
Both facts are exactly the content of Lemma~\ref{lem:restr}, which is already proved in the document: part (3) is fact (i) (restriction is symmetric monoidal, sends $G/L_+$ to coproducts of $H$-orbits, hence sends the localizing subcategory generated by the $\mathcal O$-cells into the one generated by their restrictions, and the explicit double-coset + norm-restriction computation shows each restricted/conjugated cell is again an admissible $\mathcal O|_H$-cell of cardinality $\ge n$); part (1) is fact (ii); part (2) gives $\mathrm{gconn}(i_H^*E)(H)=\mathrm{gconn}(E)(H)$. The new Step 0 of the forward proof invokes these three parts directly, so the reduction is no longer asserted but derived.

**Subgroup-by-subgroup coverage.**
This was the substantive point, and the new proof handles it correctly by making the reduction *per subgroup* rather than a single global one. For each fixed $H\subseteq G$ I set $E'=i_H^*E$ and use Lemma~\ref{lem:restr}(1)--(3) to show the target inequality $r_H\cdot\mathrm{gconn}(E)(H)\ge n$ is *identical* to the **top** inequality $[H:\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(H)]\cdot\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^H E')\ge n$ for $E'$ over $(H,\mathcal O|_H)$. The all-subgroups content is thus carried entirely by ranging $H$ over all subgroups in Step 0; what remains to prove at each $H$ is only a *top-group* ($\Phi^H$) statement. This is why inspecting only $\Phi^G$ (after renaming $H\to G$) is legitimate — the issue's worry that one must separately control $\Phi^K(G_+\wedge_{H'}X)$ for proper $K$ does not arise, because the proof never tries to verify all subgroups at once at the $G$ level; each subgroup is reduced to its own top fixed points.

For the remaining top-group statement I now give a clean 4-step argument:
- **Step 1** proves $\Phi^G(G_+\otimes_K Y)\simeq 0$ for proper $K$ from scratch, via the projection formula $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes(G_+\otimes_K Y)\simeq G_+\otimes_K(i_K^*\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes Y)$ and $i_K^*\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\simeq 0$ (since $(E\mathcal P)^K\simeq *$).
- **Step 2** shows $\mathcal C=\{X:\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^G X)\ge n/r_G\}$ is a localizing subcategory ($\Phi^G$ exact + colimit-preserving; connectivity $\ge$ a rational threshold is integer-valued hence stable under colimits/extensions/retracts/orbit tensors, using Step 1 for proper-orbit tensors).
- **Step 3** checks all generators $G_+\otimes_L N^SS^1$ lie in $\mathcal C$: proper $L$ via Step 1, and $L=G$ via Prop.~\ref{prop:phiNT} ($\Phi^G N^TS^1\simeq S^{|T/G|}$) plus Lemma~\ref{lem:index}(3) giving $r_G|T/G|\ge|T|\ge n$.
- **Step 4** concludes $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\mathcal C$ by minimality, then applies the statement to $(H,\mathcal O|_H)$.

The algebraic core $r_G|T/G|\ge|T|\ge n$ (Lemma~\ref{lem:index}(3), itself from $K\to G\Rightarrow\chi(G)\subseteq K\Rightarrow[G:K]\le r_G$) is unchanged and correct, as the issue already granted.

I verified the structural facts (projection formula, $i_K^*\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\simeq 0$, threshold-connectivity stability under colimits) and checked environment/brace balance; no LaTeX toolchain is available in the workspace to compile. I consider the gap resolved.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Follow-up: while installing the rewritten forward proof I found and fixed a definitional inconsistency that the proof depends on, so it is worth recording explicitly.

**Definitional fix (generation of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$).** The preamble of the slice-filtration section still described $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ as "the localizing subcategory ... the smallest full stable subcategory ... closed under all homotopy colimits ... [hence] (de)suspension." That is incorrect for a "$\ge n$" slice-connective subcategory: closing under desuspension $\Omega=\Sigma^{-1}$ together with colimits would make the subcategory generated by the cells equal to all of $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ (the cells generate $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ as a stable category), trivializing the filtration. I replaced this by the correct connective ("positive") generation $\langle\mathcal G\rangle_{\ge}$: smallest full subcategory containing the cells and closed under (o1) homotopy colimits (hence positive suspensions, cofibers of maps between members, extensions), (o2) retracts, (o3) orbit tensors $G/H_+\otimes(-)$ — but explicitly **not** under desuspension.

This is exactly the closure under which my Step 2/Step 4 minimality argument is valid: the subcategory $\mathcal C=\{X:\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^GX)\ge n/r_G\}$ is closed under (o1)–(o3) but is genuinely **not** desuspension-closed, which is the whole reason the forward inequality is nontrivial.

I propagated the terminology consistently: every occurrence of "localizing subcategory generated by ..." that referred to $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ or $\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$ (the definition, Lemma~\ref{lem:restr}(3)/(4), Lemma~\ref{lem:induced}, Prop.~\ref{prop:geomEM}(C), the base case) now reads "connective subcategory generated by ...", and the only remaining word "localizing" is in the definitional contrast sentence. I checked these downstream arguments only ever use the positive operations (o1)–(o3), so they remain valid verbatim; e.g. Lemma~\ref{lem:restr}(3) now notes that $i_H^*$ preserves (o1)–(o3).

Environment and brace balance verified ($744/744$ braces, no `\begin`/`\end` mismatch). The issue remains **resolved**.
